root_text.sn55.37:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sakkesu viharati kapilavatthusmiṁ nigrodhārāme.
root_text.sn55.37:1.2: Atha kho mahānāmo sakko yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho mahānāmo sakko bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn55.37:2.2: “Yato kho, mahānāma, buddhaṁ saraṇaṁ gato hoti, dhammaṁ saraṇaṁ gato hoti, saṅghaṁ saraṇaṁ gato hoti—
root_text.sn55.37:3.2: “Yato kho, mahānāma, upāsako pāṇātipātā paṭivirato hoti, adinnādānā paṭivirato hoti, kāmesumicchācārā paṭivirato hoti, musāvādā paṭivirato hoti, surāmerayamajjappamādaṭṭhānā paṭivirato hoti—
root_text.sn55.37:3.3: ettāvatā kho, mahānāma, upāsako sīlasampanno hotī”ti.
root_text.sn55.37:4.2: “Idha, mahānāma, upāsako saddho hoti, saddahati tathāgatassa bodhiṁ—
root_text.sn55.37:4.3: itipi so bhagavā …pe… satthā devamanussānaṁ buddho bhagavāti.
root_text.sn55.37:4.4: Ettāvatā kho, mahānāma, upāsako saddhāsampanno hotī”ti.
root_text.sn55.37:5.2: “Idha, mahānāma, upāsako vigatamalamaccherena cetasā agāraṁ ajjhāvasati muttacāgo payatapāṇi vossaggarato yācayogo dānasaṁvibhāgarato—
root_text.sn55.37:5.3: ettāvatā kho, mahānāma, upāsako cāgasampanno hotī”ti.
root_text.sn55.37:6.2: “Idha, mahānāma, upāsako paññavā hoti udayatthagāminiyā paññāya samannāgato ariyāya nibbedhikāya sammā dukkhakkhayagāminiyā—
root_text.sn55.37:6.3: ettāvatā kho, mahānāma, upāsako paññāsampanno hotī”ti.

translation_text.sn55.37:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying in the land of the Sakyans, near Kapilavatthu in the Banyan Tree Monastery.
translation_text.sn55.37:1.2: Then Mahānāma the Sakyan went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him:
translation_text.sn55.37:2.1: “Sir, how is a lay follower defined?”
translation_text.sn55.37:2.2: “Mahānāma, when you’ve gone for refuge to the Buddha, the teaching, and the Saṅgha,
translation_text.sn55.37:2.3: you’re considered to be a lay follower.”
translation_text.sn55.37:3.1: “But how is an ethical lay follower defined?”
translation_text.sn55.37:3.2: “When a lay follower doesn’t kill living creatures, steal, commit sexual misconduct, lie, or consume beer, wine, and liquor intoxicants,
translation_text.sn55.37:3.3: they’re considered to be an ethical lay follower.”
translation_text.sn55.37:4.1: “But how is a faithful lay follower defined?”
translation_text.sn55.37:4.2: “It’s when a lay follower has faith in the Realized One’s awakening:
translation_text.sn55.37:4.3: ‘That Blessed One is perfected, a fully awakened Buddha, accomplished in knowledge and conduct, holy, knower of the world, supreme guide for those fit for training, teacher of gods and humans, awakened, blessed.’
translation_text.sn55.37:4.4: Then they’re considered to be a faithful lay follower.”
translation_text.sn55.37:5.1: “But how is a generous lay follower defined?”
translation_text.sn55.37:5.2: “It’s when a lay follower lives at home rid of the stain of stinginess, freely generous, open-handed, loving to let go, committed to charity, loving to give and to share.
translation_text.sn55.37:5.3: Then they’re considered to be a generous lay follower.”
translation_text.sn55.37:6.1: “But how is a wise lay follower defined?”
translation_text.sn55.37:6.2: “It’s when a lay follower is wise. They have the wisdom of arising and passing away which is noble, penetrative, and leads to the complete ending of suffering.
translation_text.sn55.37:6.3: Then they’re considered to be a wise lay follower.”
